name: Wooden Church
description: Small religious building for rural areas. A humble house for faith, soft prayers and community life.

name: Eco-Office Complex
description: Glass coworking complex blending workspaces with greenery. A place to focus, produce, and be connected with nature.

name: Art Workshop
description: Former rocket factory re-purposed into artists workshops. For people who like building, repairing and gardening in a rural setting.

name: Indigenous Settlement
description: Indigenous settlement on the peripheries of The Model Nation. Placed in a protected natural area far from industrial activities.

name: Cellulose Factory
description: Industrial facility designed to extract cellulose from plant sources to produce materials such as paper, textiles or construction insulation.

name: Radio Telescope Array
description: Astronomical interferometer of 66 radio telescopes that provides insights on star birth, detailed imaging of local star and planet formation.

name: Temporary Theme Park
description: Temporary family theme park filled with attractions, cotton candy, ice cream, and lively characters.

name: Green Bunker
description: Re-purposed war shelter turned into a green complex, including a climbing gym, concert venue, techno club and public rooftop park.

name: Country House
description: Small stone house in a mountainous area, offering a quiet place for retreat, restoration, and reflection.

name: Anti-Terrorism Prison
description: High-security detention facility designed to isolate individuals considered threats to national stability.

name: Innovation Center
description: Shared workspace supporting students and entrepreneurs, combining experimental design with sustainable practices.

name: Antartic Scientific Base
description: Permanent research base supporting scientific work and logistics in extreme polar conditions.

name: Brick Office Block
description: Expressionist office building hosting businesses, education spaces, and administrative activities.

name: Huge Copper Mine
description: The largest open air copper mine in the world, guaranteeing enough raw material for electrical wiring, construction, electronics and transportation.

name: Data Center
description: Powerful colocation data center campus. Featured Tier 5® Platinum exascale data center facilities that offer a 100% power uptime guarantee.

name: TV Tower
description: Tall TV and telecommunications tower that anchors the Model Nation’s broadcasting network and stands as a landmark in its distinctive skyline.

name: Equestrian Statue To War Hero
description: Monument to an unknown soldier buried somewhere. A symbol of some old war nobody remembers.

name: Marginal Slum
description: Colorful, infinite slum construction where many people can live in the minimum possible space.

name: Atomic War Memorial
description: Preserved ruined building, a memorial of a past atomic war to remind the Model Nation that violent times passed and should never return.

name: Salpetre Mining Town
description: Nitrate mining town composed of housing, production facilities, and communal infrastructure within an arid landscape.

name: Land Art Installation
description: Urban artistic intervention raising ecological awareness. A call to the Model Nation to plant thousands of oaks and keep the land fertile.

name: Experimental Theatre Venue
description: Large-scale industrial hall repurposed as a cultural venue for performances, exhibitions, and experimental cultural productions.

name: Waled City
description: Extremely dense walled city structured through informal vertical construction, narrow circulation, and mixed residential and industrial use.

name: Presidential Palace
description: The Presidential Palace, heart of the Model Nation’s decisions, blends Neoclassical design, and gardens with orange trees.

name: Magic Theatre
description: Performance venue for children’s theatre, concerts and televised shows. A place where a magic world truly exists.

name: Shopping Mall
description: Large-scale commercial complex for The Model Nation's people to buy all they need. Credit card is the prefered payment method.

name: Cement Factory
description: Large industrial facility dedicated to cement production. A factory that brings the necessary materials for The Model Nation to continue expanding.

name: Gyros Stand
description: Gyros and Kebab store to bring delicious street food to The Model Nation.

name: Natural History Museum
description: Museum displaying ecosystems, diversity of species and geological processes shaping the Model Nation's territory.

name: National Stadium
description: Big Stadium for football matches and concerts. A place to bring people together, feel as one and cheer up The Model Nation Team.

name: Elevated Park
description: Re-purposed freight rail line turned into a linear park with pedestrian circulation, vegetation, and urban viewpoints above street level.

name: Nuclear Reactor
description: Nuclear Reactor to generate clean energy through advanced technology and strict safety protocols.

name: Oberlisk
description: Monumental vertical structure positioned in a central urban axis, functioning as a symbolic landmark and point of civic orientation.

name: Modest High-Rise
description: 14-story building where The Model Nation inhabitants can see the sunset from their homes.

name: Urban Garden
description: Community-managed green space used for growing food and cultural activities. An NGO-runned initiative that includes a wooden pop-up mini theatre.

name: Megalitic Monument
description: Monumental platform with large stone heads from the times when The Model Nation was inhabited by ancient stone-carving cultures.

name: Sambodromo
description: 700-meter-long multi-purpose open-air venue. Open to the public to dance Samba, organise endless festivals, carnivals, parades and races.

name: Olympic Bobsled Track
description: Abandoned bobsled and luge Olympic track also useful as, skate park, artillery position or trench.

name: Seed Vault
description: Secure underground facility in a remote landscape designed for long-term storage of fertile crop seeds from The Model Nation and beyond.

name: Ancient Theatre
description: Stone amphitheater carved into a hillside, evoking theatre from another time yet still functional for public performances, concerts, and plays.

name: City Park
description: Re-purposed war airport turned into a massive park for leisure activities.

name: Natural Therms
description: Geometric-shaped thermal bathing area with red linear walkways, wooden platforms, and natural pools integrated into a steep forested canyon.

name: Fairground
description: Large open urban field used for periodic mass events such as festivals, attraction parks or The Model Nation fair.

name: Memorial For A Reknowned Scientist
description: Spherical imaginary building designed as an homage to a renowned scientist who discovered the mechanics of the Model Nation’s world.

name: Walled Border
description: Reinforced steel border barrier protecting the Model Nation, equipped with surveillance systems and extending from land into the ocean.

name: War Mausoleum
description: Monumental funerary complex functioning as a burial site. Dedicated to the memory of those who lost their lives in wars from another time.

name: General Hospital
description: Large public hospital organized into specialized clinical buildings. Also used for medical training in collaboration with the national University.

name: Food Distribution Center
description: Vast logistics complex where goods flow through automated sorting, storage, and dispatch systems, linking suppliers to stores with high efficiency.

name: Trade Skyscrapers
description: Pair of high-rise office towers dedicated to manage a global network of over 500,000 businesses across The Model Nation.

name: X-Reality Lab
description: Immersive XR laboratory space equipped with projection, tracking systems, and real-time rendering infrastructure for experimental media art.
